name: Surfshark
northStar: crisp ocean horizon at golden hour — warm coral sun meets teal sea, all floating on white sand

dos: Use Inter exclusively — never introduce a secondary typeface for display or body.
Apply the three-color accent rule: teal for brand identity, coral for conversion, yellow for time-sensitive promos only.
Use 12px radius for standard buttons and 48px radius for feature cards — these two radii define the system's personality.
Maintain the dark→light→dark section rhythm: alternate between #16191c panels and #ffffff/#f9f9f9 sections at 64px gaps.
Set display headlines at 60px Inter weight 700 with -1.5px letter-spacing — tighter tracking on large sizes is non-negotiable.
Use #fa3556 filled coral buttons for the single primary action per view; switch to #16191c dark buttons for secondary actions to avoid CTA competition.
Anchor every page with the yellow promo banner above the nav when a deal is active; never move it below the fold.
donts: Don't use #1ebfbf as a button fill — teal is for brand identity, icons, and inline accent text, not for conversion surfaces.
Don't apply shadows to cards — the system relies on background contrast and 48px radius for elevation, not drop shadows.
Don't use border-radius values outside the scale: 8, 12, 32, 48, 64, 96px. Intermediate values break the visual language.
Don't place two coral CTAs in the same viewport — one coral per view, the rest dark or ghost.
Don't use the yellow #ffc200 outside promotional banners — leaking it into feature blocks dilutes its urgency signal.
Don't use serif, display, or decorative fonts — Inter at multiple weights covers the full hierarchy.
Don't set body text below 16px on marketing pages; 14px is the minimum and only for captions and micro-copy.

colors.name: Surfshark Teal
colors.role: Brand mark icon, accent highlights, inline link emphasis — the signature chromatic thread that ties logo, headings, and accent text together

colors.name: Coral Pulse
colors.role: Primary action buttons, high-urgency CTAs — warm pink-red against the cool palette creates immediate conversion pull

colors.name: Promo Gold
colors.role: Promotional banner background, limited-time deal strips — golden yellow reserved exclusively for urgency framing

colors.name: Charcoal Ink
colors.role: Primary text, dark hero backgrounds, footer surfaces, button text on coral — near-black with a hint of cool depth

colors.name: Pure White
colors.role: Page canvas, card surfaces, button text on dark fills, text on coral CTAs

colors.name: Fog White
colors.role: Alternate canvas surface, subtle card backgrounds, soft section differentiation from pure white

colors.name: Tide Tint
colors.role: Faint teal-tinted surface wash — barely-perceptible background variant for feature blocks

colors.name: Ash Gray
colors.role: Light borders, dividers, subtle separation lines — cooler-toned hairline color

colors.name: Mist Gray
colors.role: Secondary borders, disabled states, heavy-use divider color (highest neutral frequency after black)

colors.name: Slate
colors.role: Muted body text, secondary copy, icon strokes in resting state

colors.name: Graphite
colors.role: Navigation borders, tertiary UI elements, slightly lighter than Charcoal for layered depth

colors.name: Carbon
colors.role: Maximum-contrast borders, SVG icon fills, structural strokes — used heavily for crisp 1px rules

colors.name: Deep Abyss
colors.role: Alternate dark surface — slightly bluer than Charcoal, used in distinct dark panels

designSystem.layout: Max-width 1200px centered container with 64px section gaps. Hero is a full-bleed dark rounded card (64px radius) with 2-column split: text-left, image-right. Below the hero, sections alternate between centered single-column text blocks (testimonials, value props) and 2-column text+image feature panels. Navigation is a 64px-tall sticky white bar. The page rhythm is: promo banner → nav → dark hero card → white centered text → dark feature panel → white testimonial → footer. No sidebar, no mega-menu, no masonry — the layout is linear and vertically stacked with generous breathing room between sections.
designSystem.imagery: Photography is warm-toned and lifestyle-oriented: people using devices in cozy, dimly-lit domestic settings (candlelit rooms, sofas, natural evening light). Hero product renders are hyper-saturated and conceptual — a translucent teal jelly dessert with raspberries styled as VPN iconography. Avatars are circular tight crops of diverse faces used as social proof. Icons throughout are flat, single-stroke, and teal (#1ebfbf) on light backgrounds or white on dark. The overall image-to-text ratio is moderate: imagery occupies roughly 40% of viewport real estate, concentrated in hero panels and feature cards rather than full-bleed editorial spreads.

similar.why: Same dark hero card on light page pattern, teal-and-warm-accent palette, rounded 48px feature cards, Inter-style sans-serif throughout
Similar alternating dark/light section rhythm, single warm accent color against deep charcoal panels, large display headlines on dark hero backgrounds
Shared consumer-VPN visual language with rounded cards, minimal shadows, and a single chromatic action color against an otherwise neutral system
Same max-width 1200px centered layout, dark hero with split text+image, generous 64px section gaps, and Inter typography with tight display tracking

surfaces.name: Card
surfaces.purpose: Feature cards, content surfaces, light section panels

surfaces.name: Tinted Wash
surfaces.purpose: Faint teal-tinted section background for brand-adjacent blocks

surfaces.name: Dark Panel
surfaces.purpose: Hero cards, feature panels, footer — high-contrast resting surfaces

surfaces.name: Deep Abyss
surfaces.purpose: Alternate dark surface with subtle blue shift for layered depth

designSystem.northStar: crisp ocean horizon at golden hour — warm coral sun meets teal sea, all floating on white sand

components.name: Promo Banner
components.description: Full-width 40px tall bar with #ffc200 background, centered 14px Inter weight 400 black text, no border or radius. Reserved for time-sensitive offers; appears above the navigation.

components.name: Primary Navigation
components.description: White (#ffffff) background, 64px height. Left: Surfshark logo (teal
components.S\" mark + black wordmark). Center: nav items in 16px Inter weight 600 #16191c with 32px spacing. Right

components.name: Hero Card (Dark)
components.description: #16191c background with 64px border-radius, full width up to 1200px max. Left column: 60px Inter weight 700 white headline, teal #1ebfbf inline accent text at 24px, white checkmark bullet list (16px), coral CTA button. Right column: product/hero image. Generous 48–64px internal padding.

components.name: Coral Filled Button
components.description: #fa3556 background, white text, 12px border-radius, 16px vertical × 24px horizontal padding, 16px Inter weight 600. The only fully chromatic filled button in the system. Used for

components.name: Dark Filled Button
components.description: #16191c background, white text, 12px border-radius, 16px × 24px padding, 16px Inter weight 600. Used when coral would compete with a nearby primary CTA.

components.name: Checkmark Feature List
components.role: Benefit bullet list in hero/feature blocks
components.description: Teal #1ebfbf checkmark icon (16px) + 16px Inter weight 400 white or charcoal text, 8–12px row gap, left-aligned.

components.name: Feature Card (Light)
components.description: #ffffff or #f9f9f9 background, 48px border-radius, 32px padding, no shadow or 1px #dadadd border. 24–32px Inter weight 700 charcoal heading, 16px body text in #5b6065.

components.name: Feature Card (Dark)
components.description: #16191c or #1e2327 background, 48px border-radius, 32px padding. White 32px heading, #bfbfc0 or #dadadd body text, optional teal

components.name: Reviewer Avatar Strip
components.description: Row of 5–7 circular avatar images (64px diameter, full crop, no border), centered horizontally with 8–12px overlap. Small caption row beneath in 12px Inter weight 400 #5b6065.

components.name: Testimonial Block
components.description: White background, centered 32–40px Inter weight 700 #16191c quote text, 12px uppercase Inter weight 600 attribution name in #16191c, 14px #5b6065 subscriber count, dark charcoal pill button below.

components.name: Pill Tag (Rating Badge)
components.#16191c text: no background. Part of trust-bar row above content."

components.name: Footer
components.description: #16191c or near-black background, white 16px link columns organized under bold 14px Inter weight 700 white headings, 32px column gap, 48px vertical padding. Logo repeats in white/teal at top.

components.name: Pill Button (Dark)
components.role: Compact secondary CTA in testimonial/mid-page zones
components.description: #16191c background, white 16px Inter weight 600 text, 96px border-radius (full pill), 12px × 24px padding. Used for

typography.role: Sole typeface across the entire product — 400 for body and descriptions, 600 for navigation, subheadings, and emphasis, 700 for display headlines and button labels. Inter's geometric neutrality and tall x-height keep the system legible at 12px captions while the 60px display weight holds authority without requiring a custom display face.
typography.letterSpacing: -1.5px at 60px (-0.025em), near-zero at body sizes, +0.24px at 14px (0.017em) for micro-copy

designSystem.description: Surfshark's design language is a confident light-mode consumer SaaS anchored by a vivid teal brand mark and a warm coral primary action. Dark charcoal hero cards and feature panels provide high-contrast resting places between airy white sections, creating a rhythm of dark→light→dark that frames the product as both trustworthy and modern. Typography is exclusively Inter with a wide size range (12–60px) and a slight negative tracking at display sizes that tightens headlines without feeling clinical. Components lean rounded and pill-like — 48px card radii, 12px button radii — giving the interface a friendly, approachable feel. The three-color accent system (teal for brand identity, coral for conversion, yellow for promotional urgency) keeps the chromatic footprint tiny but high-impact, while a deep neutral scale handles 95% of the UI.

customSections.title: Agent Prompt Guide
customSections.content:
primary action: #fa3556 (filled action)
Create a Primary Action Button: #fa3556 background, #ffffff text, 9999px radius, compact pill padding. Use this filled treatment for the main CTA.

designSystem.elevationPhilosophy: The system avoids drop shadows entirely. Elevation is communicated through background-color contrast (white card on #f9f9f9 canvas, or #16191c panel on white section), 48px border-radius on large surfaces, and 1px hairline borders in #dadadd or #bfbfc0. This keeps the interface flat, fast, and modern — shadows would add visual weight that conflicts with the airy consumer-SaaS tone.
